definition_population.content: Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing pancreatic beta cells causing absolute insulin deficiency; classically pediatric-onset but increasingly diagnosed in adults.

sources.quote: Although type 1 diabetes mellitus (T1DM) is traditionally viewed as a youth-onset disorder, the number of older adults being diagnosed with this disease is growing.

epidemiology.content: Global incidence rising 2-5%/year; US prevalence ~1 in 300 by age 18. Growing recognition of adult-onset/older-adult T1D with a comparable incidence peak.

sources.quote: the incidence of T1D has been increasing by 2% to 5% worldwide and that the prevalence of T1D is approximately 1 in 300 in the United States by 18 years of age
Although type 1 diabetes mellitus (T1DM) is traditionally viewed as a youth-onset disorder, the number of older adults being diagnosed with this disease is growing.

biology.content: Classic model: autoreactive T cells destroy beta cells. Alternative model: intrinsic beta-cell stress provokes immune attack. DIPK1A/FAM69A is a predicted calcium-regulated atypical kinase; only literature found concerns neurological disorders, not T1D or T-cell biology — no direct link established.

sources.quote: We propose an alternative view in which the β-cell is the key contributor to the disease. By their nature and function, β-cells are prone to biosynthetic stress with limited measures for self-defence.
Using bioinformatics tools, we predicted protein kinase structure for proteins coded by five related human genes and their Metazoan homologues, the FAM69 family

treatment.content: Standard of care is lifelong exogenous insulin. FDA-approved teplizumab (TZIELD, BLA761183) delays T1D onset in at-risk individuals as a CD3-directed monoclonal antibody.

sources.quote: TEPLIZUMAB-MZWV, brand TZIELD, BLA761183, original approval action AP dated 2022-11-17 [application BLA761183; no URL field returned by drug-regulatory MCP tool -- no URL constructed]

regulatory_trials.content: 3,640 total interventional trials for T1D on ClinicalTrials.gov. Example Phase 2 beta-cell preservation trial: NCT04545151.

sources.quote: A Randomised, Double-blind, Placebo Controlled, Parallel Group, Multi-centre Trial in Adult Subjects With Newly Diagnosed Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus Investigating the Effect of Verapamil SR on Preservation of Beta-cell Function

gaps: No direct PubMed evidence linking DIPK1A/FAM69A to T1D or CD4+ T-cell biology (0 hits on direct search); only retrieved paper concerns neurological disorders.
Insulin regimen/standard-of-care literature not independently re-sourced (well established, out of scope for compact pass).
